name: ExtensionDeviceRequest_DoNotPerform
title: R5: True if the request is to stop or not to start using the device (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` 0..1 `boolean`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` 0..1 `boolean`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` has a context of DeviceRequest based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DeviceRequest`.
Element `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: True if the request is to stop or not to start using the device (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` has a context of DeviceRequest based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DeviceRequest`.
Element `DeviceRequest.doNotPerform` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If do not perform is not specified, the request is a positive request e.g. "do perform". DeviceRequest.reasonCode includes the reason for marking the DeviceRequest as 'do not perform'.
element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because this element negates the request to occur (ie, this is a request for the medication not to be ordered or prescribed, etc.)

element.short: True if the request is to stop or not to start using the device
element.definition: If true, indicates that the provider is asking for the patient to either stop using or to not start using the specified device or category of devices. For example, the patient has undergone surgery and the provider is indicating that the patient should not wear contact lenses.
element.comment: If do not perform is not specified, the request is a positive request e.g. "do perform". DeviceRequest.reasonCode includes the reason for marking the DeviceRequest as 'do not perform'.
